reasoning: The informal statement says: if X is an infinite set, then (the whole space) X is connected in the cofinite topology. The corrected formalization captures this as `[Infinite X] → IsConnected (Set.univ : Set (CofiniteTopology X))`. The original formalization instead asserts `∀ (s : Set (CofiniteTopology X)), Infinite s → IsConnected s`, i.e., every infinite subset of any X (with cofinite topology) is connected as a subspace. Logically, the original is strictly stronger than the corrected: specializing s = univ and using that Infinite univ ↔ Infinite X recovers the corrected theorem, but the corrected doesn't imply the original (it says nothing about arbitrary subsets). The original statement is actually provably true: in the cofinite topology on X, the subspace topology on any subset s is the cofinite topology on s, and any infinite set with its cofinite topology is connected (while if X is finite, no subset is infinite so the implication is vacuous). The formalizer misread the informal statement — replacing "X is connected" with "every infinite subset is connected" — which is a misunderstanding of what the mathematical statement asks for.
